code:
import io
import string

fn parse_two(_ s: String) -> Result<Int, String>
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut nums = [0, 0]
  mut idx = 0
  while idx < 2
    while i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(b)
        if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
          i = i + 1
        else
          i = n + 1
        end
      when None
        i = n
      end
      if i == n + 1
        i = i - 1
        i = n
      end
    end
    if i >= n
      return Err("not enough")
    end
    mut sign = 1
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 45
        sign = -1
        i = i + 1
      else if b == 43
        i = i + 1
      end
    when None
      return Err("eof")
    end
    mut val = 0
    mut got = false
    while i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(b)
        if b >= 48 and b <= 57
          val = val * 10 + (b - 48)
          got = true
          i = i + 1
        else
          i = n + 1
        end
      when None
        i = n
      end
      if i == n + 1
        i = n
        i = i
      end
    end
    if not got
      return Err("no digit")
    end
    if idx == 0
      nums = [val * sign, 0]
    else
      let a = match nums.get(0) when Some(x) x when None 0 end
      nums = [a, val * sign]
    end
    idx = idx + 1
  end
  let a = match nums.get(0) when Some(x) x when None 0 end
  let b = match nums.get(1) when Some(x) x when None 0 end
  Ok(a * a + b * b)
end

let input = io.read_to_end()
match parse_two(input)
when Ok(r)
  print("r=#{r}")
when Err(_)
  print("r=0")
end
